meta.title: EU Tech Sovereignty Universe
meta.context: Companies that could form the EU's sovereign technology stack, mapped to the European Technological Sovereignty Package (adopted 3 June 2026: Chips Act 2.0, Cloud & AI Development Act, Open Source Strategy).
meta.fx_note: Listed market caps & multiples are point-in-time via yfinance/Yahoo Finance on 2026-06-05; non-EUR converted at EUR=USD 1.1585 / GBP 0.8633 / SEK 10.90 / NOK 10.88 / CHF 0.918. Revenues are latest reported FY (FY ends differ: Infineon=Sep, Soitec=Mar, Eutelsat=Jun). size_eur = market cap (listed) or last valuation / funding / revenue (private); null => default node size.
meta.disclaimer: Informational only. NOT investment advice. Figures are point-in-time and may be stale; verify live quotes and primary filings before any decision.
meta.scope: Scoped to the four instruments of the European Technological Sovereignty Package (proposed 3 June 2026). Domains outside the package (defence, space, quantum, connectivity, enterprise software) are intentionally excluded.

instruments.name: Communication on European Tech Sovereignty
instruments.summary: The political umbrella of the package: the EU's strategy to cut strategic tech dependencies and back trusted European technologies.
proposes: Sets the direction for the four instruments below

instruments.name: Chips Act 2.0
instruments.summary: Builds on the 2023 Chips Act to strengthen the EU semiconductor value chain and reduce dependency on third countries.
proposes: Reinforce European strengths incl. mainstream chips
Build capacity in cutting-edge semiconductor technologies
Reduce dependency in advanced manufacturing & design

instruments.name: Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA)
instruments.summary: Strengthens EU sovereignty in cloud and AI: scale data-centre capacity, back frontier/industrial AI, and assess cloud sovereignty.
proposes: Triple EU data-centre capacity in 5–7 years (faster permitting; access to energy, land, water, financing)
Support next-gen, frontier and industrial AI + public-sector adoption
A 4-level cloud-sovereignty framework, joint procurement and 'open-source-first'

instruments.name: EU Open Source Strategy
instruments.summary: Promotes open source for digital sovereignty — transparent, reusable, interoperable public-sector software with less vendor lock-in.
proposes: Embed open source in public tenders + long-term maintenance
Strengthen the EU Public Sector OSPO network and shared assets
Common security baselines for open-source repositories

instruments.name: Strategic Roadmap for Digitalisation & AI in Energy
instruments.summary: Roadmap to digitalise the energy system and apply AI to grids, efficiency, flexibility and power markets.
proposes: Accelerate digitalisation of the energy system
Deploy AI across grids, efficiency and energy markets

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.description: The world's sole supplier of extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) lithography systems and a leader in deep-UV (DUV) scanners, metrology and installed-base services. Its machines pattern the most advanced logic and memory chips on Earth.
companies.sovereignty_role: The single most strategic chokepoint in global semiconductor manufacturing and Europe's crown jewel: no leading-edge chip can be made at scale without ASML's EUV tools.

companies.name: ASM International
companies.description: A leading supplier of wafer-processing equipment, dominant in atomic layer deposition (ALD) used to build gate-all-around and advanced logic transistors. Sells deposition and epitaxy tools to the world's chipmakers.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading deposition-equipment maker and an oligopoly leader in ALD, a process critical to every advanced-node fab.

companies.name: BE Semiconductor (Besi)
companies.description: Maker of semiconductor assembly equipment, especially die-attach and hybrid-bonding tools used in advanced packaging and chiplet integration for AI processors. A near-monopoly in hybrid bonding.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leader in advanced-packaging/hybrid-bonding equipment, an increasingly decisive step for AI-chip performance.

companies.name: AIXTRON
companies.description: Supplier of metal-organic chemical vapour deposition (MOCVD) and other epitaxy systems for compound semiconductors (GaN, SiC, optoelectronics, power and laser devices).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's compound-semiconductor deposition-equipment champion, enabling GaN/SiC power and photonics manufacturing in Europe.

companies.name: Soitec
companies.description: Maker of engineered substrates (FD-SOI, RF-SOI, Power-SOI, SmartSiC, Photonics-SOI) using its SmartCut layer-transfer technology. Sells wafers to foundries and IDMs; near-monopoly in SOI.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's near-monopoly substrate supplier (RF-SOI for smartphones, FD-SOI for edge AI, Photonics-SOI for AI datacentre optics); upstream enabler of the chip value chain.

companies.name: Siltronic AG
companies.description: One of the world's few makers of hyperpure 200mm/300mm silicon wafers, the base substrate of almost all semiconductors, supplied under long-term contracts from fabs in Germany, the US and Singapore.
companies.sovereignty_role: The principal EU-headquartered silicon-wafer supplier and one of only a handful worldwide, a foundational upstream node for European chip self-sufficiency.

companies.name: Carl Zeiss SMT
companies.description: The ZEISS division that designs and builds the high-performance optics at the heart of ASML's DUV and EUV/High-NA lithography scanners, plus photomask and process-control tools. Sole supplier of EUV projection optics to ASML.
companies.sovereignty_role: An irreplaceable single-source chokepoint inside the only machines that can pattern leading-edge chips; with ASML it gives Europe ownership of the most critical capability in chipmaking.
uncertain: Foundation-owned, not VC-funded; revenue ~€5.06bn & ~9,349 staff are for the full ZEISS SMT segment (FY2024/25); no public valuation.

companies.name: PVA TePla
companies.description: Maker of crystal-growing systems (silicon and SiC) and semiconductor metrology (GHz acoustic microscopy for void/TSV inspection used in HBM and advanced packaging).
companies.sovereignty_role: A niche EU equipment/metrology supplier into the SiC and advanced-packaging supply chains the Chips Act is trying to seed in Europe.

companies.name: LPKF Laser & Electronics
companies.description: Maker of laser-based manufacturing systems; its LIDE process makes glass-core substrates and through-glass vias for advanced AI/HPC chip packaging, alongside PCB-prototyping, welding and solar tools.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European equipment maker positioned for glass-core substrates, an emerging high-value node in AI-chip advanced packaging.

companies.name: STMicroelectronics
companies.description: A broad-line integrated device manufacturer (IDM) in power & analog, microcontrollers (STM32), MEMS/sensors, SiC and RF. Owns fabs in France, Italy, Singapore; FD-SOI and full SiC value chain are distinctive.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of Europe's two flagship chipmakers and the leader in SiC power and FD-SOI, with EU-funded fabs (Crolles, Catania) central to Chips Act capacity.

companies.name: Infineon Technologies
companies.description: Global #1 in power semiconductors and automotive chips, an IDM spanning Si/SiC/GaN power, AURIX auto MCUs, sensors, and connectivity/security (ex-Cypress). Fabs in Dresden, Villach, Kulim.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's largest chipmaker and #1 in power & automotive semis; its EU/state-aid-backed Dresden megafab and AI-datacentre power push are core to EU sovereignty.

companies.name: NXP Semiconductors
companies.description: A leading supplier of microcontrollers, application processors, analog, RF, security and connectivity chips (co-inventor of NFC), focused on automotive, industrial/IoT and comms. Dutch-domiciled in Eindhoven.
companies.sovereignty_role: The largest EU-headquartered chipmaker and dominant European supplier of automotive and secure-edge silicon, though it is listed only on Nasdaq (not an EU exchange).
uncertain: Dutch HQ but US-listed (Nasdaq, USD); no EU listing.

companies.name: X-FAB Silicon Foundries
companies.description: A specialty / analog-mixed-signal foundry on mature nodes (analog, high-voltage BCD, SiC, MEMS, photonics), serving automotive, industrial and medical customers from fabs in France, Germany, the US and Malaysia.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's specialty foundry and a direct IPCEI ME/CT recipient (up to €80m), adding sovereign analog/SiC/MEMS capacity.

companies.name: Nordic Semiconductor
companies.description: A fabless leader in ultra-low-power Bluetooth Low Energy SoCs, also supplying cellular IoT and Wi-Fi connectivity. Its chips power a large share of the world's wireless IoT, wearable and smart-home devices.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's homegrown leader in low-power wireless connectivity silicon, reducing reliance on US/Asian connectivity-chip vendors.

companies.name: Melexis
companies.description: A fabless maker of automotive sensor ICs (magnetic position/current, Hall, time-of-flight) with strong design-in share. Manufactures largely through foundry partner X-FAB.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European automotive-sensor leader, though capital-light and structurally outside direct chip-subsidy flows.

companies.name: OVHcloud
companies.description: Europe's largest cloud-infrastructure provider (IaaS, bare-metal, hosted private cloud) operating its own datacentres and servers. A sovereign-cloud pure-play and GAIA-X founder.
companies.sovereignty_role: The purest listed European sovereign-cloud play; part of the EC's €180m sovereign-cloud framework award and a credible alternative to US hyperscalers.

companies.name: IONOS Group
companies.description: European web-hosting and cloud provider (domains, hosting, IONOS Cloud IaaS), majority-owned by United Internet, serving SMBs and the public sector with ~6.6m customers.
companies.sovereignty_role: A German/European hosting and cloud provider positioned as a sovereign alternative to US hyperscalers for public-sector and SME workloads.

companies.name: Atos / Eviden
companies.description: French IT-services group providing managed services, cloud and cybersecurity. Its Bull/BullSequana HPC, quantum and AI-computing business was sold to the French State (completed Apr 2026); Atos completed a major 2024 debt restructuring and is executing the multi-year 'Genesis' turnaround on a reduced perimeter.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European cybersecurity, managed-services and sovereign-cloud provider important to French/EU digital sovereignty; its supercomputing (Bull) sovereignty role has moved to the now state-owned Bull entity. Remains under financial restructuring.
uncertain: Severe financial distress / 'Genesis' restructuring; Bull/HPC divested to the French State (Apr 2026) so the group no longer owns supercomputing; FY2025 figures preliminary.

companies.name: STACKIT (Schwarz Group)
companies.description: German sovereign cloud provider, the cloud arm of Schwarz Digits (Schwarz Group, owner of Lidl/Kaufland). Operates datacentres in Germany and Austria; expanding into a full 'German hyperscaler' for enterprise and public administration.
companies.sovereignty_role: A flagship fully German-owned sovereign cloud; cooperating with the BSI on public-administration cloud and hosting sovereign SAP/AI workloads (RISE, Aleph Alpha's PhariaAI).
uncertain: No standalone revenue/valuation disclosed (parent Schwarz Digits ~€1.9bn division revenue).

companies.name: Scaleway
companies.description: French public-cloud provider, wholly owned by the iliad Group, offering IaaS, bare-metal and GPU/AI cloud (5,000+ GPUs) across European regions. Customers include Hugging Face.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European-owned public/AI cloud and a pillar of iliad's €3bn cloud & AI programme, supplying GPU capacity to European AI developers.
uncertain: No standalone revenue/valuation disclosed; ~639 employees.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.description: Paris-based developer of open-weight large language models (Mistral/Mixtral) and the Le Chat assistant, offering frontier and small open models, enterprise APIs and on-prem/sovereign deployments.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship sovereign foundation-model champion and the principal European alternative to US frontier-AI labs; central to the EU's AI sovereignty agenda.

companies.name: Aleph Alpha
companies.description: Heidelberg-based enterprise/sovereign AI company building explainable LLMs (Luminous/Pharia) and the PhariaAI platform for governments and regulated industries, with on-prem data-sovereign deployments.
companies.sovereignty_role: Germany's headline sovereign-AI company, targeting public sector and defence; backed by the Schwarz Group as a strategic sovereignty play.
uncertain: Standalone valuation effectively undisclosed; pending Cohere merger (announced Apr 2026, not closed).

companies.name: DeepL
companies.description: Cologne-based neural machine-translation company (DeepL Translator, Write, Voice) across 30+ languages, widely regarded as best-in-class on translation quality.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European language-AI leader and credible homegrown alternative to US translation big-tech for GDPR-compliant, EU-based language processing.
uncertain: Latest priced round May 2024; may be stale.

companies.name: AT&S
companies.description: Leading European maker of high-end printed circuit boards and IC substrates for semiconductors (mobile, automotive, AI/HPC), with plants in Austria, China, India and Korea.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's principal IC-substrate / advanced-PCB maker - the chip-to-system packaging layer that Chips Act 2.0 flags as a European supply-chain gap.
uncertain: Much substrate capacity is China-located; sovereignty contribution is HQ/IP plus Austrian production.

companies.name: SUSS MicroTec SE
companies.description: Garching-based semiconductor-equipment maker: photolithography (coater/developer), bonding and photomask-cleaning systems for chip, MEMS and advanced-packaging manufacturing.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European front-end/advanced-packaging equipment supplier strengthening the EU fab-equipment base alongside ASML/Aixtron under Chips Act 2.0.

companies.name: Black Forest Labs
companies.description: Freiburg-based generative-AI lab and Europe's leading image/visual foundation-model company (the FLUX models), founded by former Stability AI researchers behind Stable Diffusion.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship open/visual foundation-model developer - a sovereign counterweight in generative-media AI alongside Mistral on language.
uncertain: USD-reported ($300M Series B at $3.25bn, Dec 2025); EUR approx.

companies.name: H Company
companies.description: Paris AI lab (formerly Holistic AI) building agentic AI models that automate business processes; raised a ~$220M seed in 2024, among Europe's largest.
companies.sovereignty_role: A French sovereign frontier-AI lab focused on autonomous agents, backed by leading European industrialists; part of France's bid for independent AI capability.
uncertain: ~$220M seed (USD), EUR approx; no public valuation; cap table changed after 2024 co-founder departures.

companies.name: Jenoptik AG
companies.description: German photonics group; its Advanced Photonic Solutions arm supplies precision optics, lasers and metrology used in semiconductor lithography and wafer inspection.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European supplier of precision optics/photonic subsystems into the semiconductor capital-equipment chain, reducing reliance on US/Japanese optics.
uncertain: Semiconductor optics is one segment of a broader group (also mobility/metrology).

companies.name: Elmos Semiconductor SE
companies.description: Dortmund-based designer of automotive mixed-signal semiconductors (sensor ICs, motor/LED drivers, system ICs) for airbags, lighting, access and comfort electronics.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of the few European-owned automotive-semiconductor specialists, supporting EU auto chip supply security alongside Infineon, NXP, STMicro and Melexis.

companies.name: ams-OSRAM AG
companies.description: Austrian-headquartered optical-semiconductor and sensor group (LEDs, VCSEL lasers, photonic and CMOS sensor ICs) for automotive, consumer, industrial and medical markets; formed from the 2020 ams/OSRAM merger.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship optical-semiconductor and sensing player (automotive lighting, 3D sensing, photonics), reducing reliance on Asian/US emitter and sensor suppliers.
uncertain: Austria-HQ (EU) but PRIMARY LISTING is SIX Swiss in CHF; market cap converted from CHF. Loss-making FY2025 (net loss ~EUR188m) so P/E n/a.

companies.name: SiPearl
companies.description: French fabless designer of energy-efficient HPC microprocessors; its Arm-based Rhea CPU family powers the European Processor Initiative and Europe's exascale supercomputers.
companies.sovereignty_role: Cornerstone of the EU's effort to build a sovereign high-performance-compute processor, reducing dependence on US x86/GPU vendors for supercomputing and AI.
uncertain: Private; no public valuation — total funding (~EUR130m) used as size proxy.

companies.name: SMART Photonics
companies.description: Eindhoven-based independent pure-play foundry for indium-phosphide (InP) photonic integrated circuits; a central node in the Dutch/European integrated-photonics ecosystem (PhotonDelta).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading open-access InP photonic-chip foundry, underpinning sovereign integrated-photonics capability for telecom, sensing and AI interconnect.
uncertain: Private; no public valuation — total funding (~EUR220m) used as size proxy.

companies.name: CANCOM SE
companies.description: German IT-services and hybrid/multi-cloud provider running a Cloud Marketplace that integrates sovereign European clouds (STACKIT, IONOS) alongside hyperscalers, plus managed services and cybersecurity.
companies.sovereignty_role: A German integrator channelling public-sector and regulated workloads onto sovereign European clouds; a distribution layer for EU Cloud & AI Development Act-compliant infrastructure.

companies.name: Northern Data AG
companies.description: German HPC/AI-infrastructure group operating Taiga Cloud (NVIDIA H100/H200 GPU cloud, ~24k GPUs) and Ardent data centres; an NVIDIA-designated sovereign-AI cloud provider.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of Europe's largest sovereign AI-cloud / GPU-infrastructure operators, relevant to the EU Cloud & AI Development Act's compute-sovereignty goals.
uncertain: Distressed/speculative: FY2025 revenue only ~EUR80m (-34%), large net loss; revenue lags the deployed-capacity narrative.

companies.name: LightOn
companies.description: Paris-based enterprise generative-AI company; its Paradigm platform delivers on-prem/sovereign GenAI (LLMs, RAG, agents) to corporates and the public sector. First pure-play GenAI company listed in Europe (IPO Nov 2024).
companies.sovereignty_role: The only listed European pure-play sovereign generative-AI vendor, targeting GDPR-compliant on-prem deployments for French/EU government and regulated enterprises.
uncertain: Micro-cap (~EUR24m), down heavily from IPO; only market cap verified, other financials left null.

companies.name: Kyutai
companies.description: Paris-based NON-PROFIT open-science AI research lab (behind the Moshi speech model); releases open models, code and papers. Funded by a ~EUR300m 5-year commitment from Iliad/Xavier Niel, CMA CGM and Schmidt Sciences.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship open-science AI lab and a sovereign open-research counterweight to closed US labs; positioned alongside (and distinct from) Mistral as fully open.
uncertain: NON-PROFIT lab, not a company — no valuation/cap table; the EUR300m is a 5-year funding commitment used for sizing only.

companies.name: Qt Group Oyj
companies.description: Finnish software company that develops and commercialises the Qt cross-platform application development framework, offered under both commercial and open-source (GPL/LGPL) licenses.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European-controlled open-source software foundation for GUIs and embedded systems, reducing reliance on US-controlled UI/dev toolchains; its dual open-source/commercial model fits the EU Open Source Strategy's asset-reuse and reduced-vendor-lock-in goals.
uncertain: Employee count 1,136 (incl. 215 from IAR acquisition) is year-end 2025 per company report relayed via press, not a direct IR PDF

companies.name: SUSE
companies.description: One of the oldest enterprise Linux vendors (SUSE Linux Enterprise, openSUSE, Rancher/Kubernetes). Listed on Frankfurt 2021-2023, then taken private by EQT and reincorporated as a Luxembourg S.A. with operations centred in Nuremberg, Germany.
companies.sovereignty_role: A flagship European enterprise open-source vendor repositioning around digital sovereignty (sovereign cloud/Linux alternatives to US hyperscaler stacks), directly relevant to the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: ~EUR5.5bn is a 2026 sale price tag reported via EQT advisor process, not a closed-transaction valuation
Revenue not disclosed since going private (third-party ARR estimates ~$298M)
Total funding not cleanly disclosed

companies.name: Odoo SA
companies.description: Belgian developer of the open-source Odoo all-in-one business management/ERP suite, combining a free community edition with paid Enterprise features.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European open-source ERP offering an EU alternative to US proprietary business software (SAP/Microsoft/Oracle); its open-source core and self-hostable model align with the EU Open Source Strategy's reduced-lock-in and asset-reuse aims.
uncertain: EUR7bn (Jan 2026) is a secondary-transaction valuation, not a primary raise
Reported revenue (~EUR282M 2023) are press figures, not audited filings

companies.name: Nextcloud GmbH
companies.description: German developer of Nextcloud, the leading self-hosted open-source content-collaboration/file-sync platform (files, chat, groupware), released fully under open-source licenses.
companies.sovereignty_role: Markets itself as 'the sovereign answer to Big Tech', letting public bodies keep data on-premises under their own control; a core reference for the EU Open Source Strategy's public-sector OSS and anti-lock-in objectives.
uncertain: Employee count ~100-143 varies by source; left null
Employee/organically funded with no significant VC valuation disclosed; valuation and funding null

companies.name: Univention GmbH
companies.description: Bremen-based developer of Univention Corporate Server (UCS) and Nubus, open-source (AGPLv3) identity & access management and IT-infrastructure platforms widely used in German public administration and education.
companies.sovereignty_role: Brands itself as making 'digital sovereignty a reality'; provides EU-controlled open-source IAM/portal alternatives to Microsoft Active Directory/Entra for public sector and schools.
uncertain: Employee count (LinkedIn band 51-200) not precisely confirmed
No disclosed external valuation or VC funding

companies.name: XWiki SAS
companies.description: Paris-based company behind the open-source XWiki collaborative wiki/knowledge platform and the end-to-end-encrypted CryptPad suite (LGPL/AGPL).
companies.sovereignty_role: Recipient of France 2030 cloud-strategy funding to build French/European open-source collaborative suites as sovereign alternatives to US collaboration tools; aligned with the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: ~EUR2.4M is France 2030 consortium grant funding over ~4 years, not equity venture funding

companies.name: Centreon
companies.description: Paris-based developer of the open-source Centreon IT infrastructure monitoring and observability platform, used in 60+ countries.
companies.sovereignty_role: A French open-source observability vendor positioning as 'the leading European observability platform', an EU-controlled alternative to US monitoring SaaS.
uncertain: Sixth Street investment (Nov 2025, first external fundraise) amount and valuation not disclosed
~160 employees / ~1,300 customers per press, not audited

companies.name: OpenProject GmbH
companies.description: Berlin-based company behind OpenProject, an open-source (GPLv3) web-based project-management suite (classic, agile, hybrid) with enterprise support and hosting.
companies.sovereignty_role: Self-hosted German open-source PM tool promoted as a sovereign 'made in Germany' alternative to US SaaS (e.g. Jira), featured on the EU's Open Source Observatory.
uncertain: No disclosed external valuation or VC funding

companies.name: Schneider Electric SE
companies.description: French multinational in energy management and industrial automation; its EcoStruxure platform spans grid software (ADMS/DERMS) and building/industrial energy optimisation, with embedded AI (Grid AI Assistant, AI-driven outage restoration).
companies.sovereignty_role: A core European provider of digital-grid and energy-management software with AI features, directly relevant to the roadmap's goals of digitalising the energy system and applying AI to grids, efficiency and flexibility.
uncertain: Schneider is a large diversified electrical/automation industrial — total revenue (~EUR38bn, +8% organic FY2024) is NOT a proxy for AI-in-energy exposure; only a portion is energy-software/AI
Employee count 155,822 (2024) varies by source

companies.name: Siemens Energy AG
companies.description: German energy-technology group spun off from Siemens (listed 28 Sep 2020); its Grid Technologies business supplies high-voltage transmission, HVDC and digital-grid solutions, with AI investment to strengthen grid resilience.
companies.sovereignty_role: A leading European grid-technology supplier whose Grid Technologies unit and digital/AI grid solutions are central to the roadmap's focus on grids and energy-system digitalisation.
uncertain: Large diversified energy-hardware industrial — only a portion (Grid Technologies + digital) is grid-software/AI; total revenue not a proxy for AI-in-energy exposure
AI/digital-grid revenue not separately disclosed

companies.name: Volue ASA
companies.description: Norwegian software and data company for energy and infrastructure: power-market forecasting/trading software, grid (DSO flexibility) management and water/infrastructure solutions, increasingly SaaS. Delisted from the Oslo Stock Exchange on 28 Oct 2024 after a buyout.
companies.sovereignty_role: A specialised European power-market and grid software vendor whose forecasting, flexibility and grid-management tools map directly onto the roadmap's AI-for-grids, flexibility and power-markets priorities.
uncertain: Delisted from Oslo Bors 28 Oct 2024 — no longer publicly listed
~EUR516m valuation converted from NOK 42/share offer (~NOK 6bn) at ~0.086 EUR/NOK; FX-dependent, approximate
FY2024 operating revenue ~NOK 1,634m (~EUR140m, +12%) per owner disclosure

companies.name: METRON
companies.description: Paris-based cleantech offering an AI/data-science SaaS energy management and optimisation platform (EMOS) for industrial sites, including the METRON-EVA virtual assistant for real-time energy optimisation.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European AI-for-energy software pure-play focused on industrial energy efficiency and optimisation, aligned with the roadmap's emphasis on applying AI to energy efficiency and flexibility.
uncertain: Total funding ~EUR52.5m (energystartups.org); other sources ~USD55.5m — approximate
No disclosed post-money valuation; investor list a non-exhaustive subset

companies.name: gridX
companies.description: German energy-management software company (Aachen/Munich) whose XENON IoT platform connects, monitors and controls distributed energy resources (PV, EV chargers, batteries, heat pumps). Wholly owned by energy group E.ON since 2022.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European DER/energy-management software platform enabling load flexibility and smart-grid integration of distributed assets, relevant to the roadmap's grid-digitalisation and flexibility goals.
uncertain: Now a wholly owned subsidiary of E.ON (full ownership by Dec 2022) — no longer an independent startup
Total funding and valuation not reliably disclosed

companies.name: Tibber
companies.description: Norwegian smart-energy company offering a consumer app with dynamic hourly electricity pricing and a residential virtual power plant integrating EV chargers, heat pumps and batteries to optimise household consumption.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European consumer-side flexibility/VPP and smart-energy software player aggregating distributed demand for grid balancing, supporting the roadmap's flexibility and energy-digitalisation objectives.
uncertain: HQ registered in Forde, Norway with large hub in Stockholm, Sweden — both EU/EEA
Total funding ~USD177m over 4 rounds (Tracxn); EUR total not cleanly disclosed
No disclosed post-money valuation

companies.name: Sympower
companies.description: Amsterdam-based independent flexibility/virtual power plant operator whose software aggregates and optimises distributed assets (incl. battery storage) for demand response and frequency reserves, controlling multiple GW across Europe.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of Europe's leading independent VPP/flexibility software operators, aligned with the roadmap's priorities on flexibility, grid balancing and applying software/AI to power markets.
uncertain: Total funding ~USD81m over 12 rounds (Tracxn); cumulative EUR total not cleanly disclosed
No disclosed post-money valuation; key investors not reliably identified — left empty
'AI' angle is via forecasting/optimisation software (partly inferred)

companies.name: Proxmox Server Solutions GmbH
companies.description: Vienna-based vendor of open-source enterprise server software — Proxmox Virtual Environment (virtualization), Backup Server and Mail Gateway, all under GNU AGPLv3.
companies.sovereignty_role: A fully open-source, self-hostable alternative to proprietary virtualization (e.g. VMware), supporting the EU Open Source Strategy's reduced-lock-in and public-sector sovereignty goals.
uncertain: Appears bootstrapped/self-financed; no funding or valuation data found

companies.name: Open-Xchange AG
companies.description: German open-source company (Cologne/Nuremberg) behind OX App Suite email/collaboration and, via its 2015 Dovecot merger, the Dovecot IMAP server and PowerDNS; powers hosted email for hundreds of millions of users.
companies.sovereignty_role: Supplies open-source email/groupware/DNS used by European telcos as a sovereign alternative to US hyperscaler email — aligned with the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: Current valuation/total funding not verified; investor list from secondary sources

companies.name: Enalean SAS
companies.description: French company (near Chambéry) developing Tuleap, an open-source (GPLv2) application-lifecycle and agile project-management platform.
companies.sovereignty_role: Tuleap is an open-source European alternative to Atlassian (Jira/Confluence), reducing dependence on proprietary US SaaS — aligned with the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: Appears self-funded; no external funding/valuation identified

companies.name: Zammad GmbH
companies.description: Berlin-based company offering Zammad, an open-source web-based helpdesk/ticketing system; source code owned by the independent Zammad Foundation while the GmbH provides hosting/support.
companies.sovereignty_role: A GDPR-compliant, self-hostable open-source helpdesk as a European alternative to proprietary SaaS (e.g. Zendesk) — supports the EU Open Source Strategy's data-sovereignty aims.
uncertain: Founding 2014 (project) vs 2015 (legal entity)
Appears bootstrapped

companies.name: LINAGORA
companies.description: French open-source software publisher (Issy-les-Moulineaux) developing the Twake Workplace collaboration suite and OBM groupware, plus open-source AI work.
companies.sovereignty_role: Markets Twake as a 100% open-source sovereign European alternative to Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace — directly serving the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: Funding/valuation not disclosed; Groupe Chevrillon is a shareholder, stake unverified

companies.name: Passbolt SA
companies.description: Luxembourg-based company behind Passbolt, an open-source, self-hostable password and secrets manager for team collaboration.
companies.sovereignty_role: An EU-based self-hostable open-source password/secrets manager — a sovereign alternative to US password managers, supporting the EU Open Source Strategy.
uncertain: Total funding ~EUR13.1M approximate (converted from ~$14.1M cumulative); valuation undisclosed

companies.name: Kalray
companies.description: Fabless semiconductor company (CEA spin-off, Grenoble) designing manycore DPU processors and acceleration solutions for intensive data processing from cloud to edge.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of few EU-HQ fabless designers of high-performance data-processing silicon, relevant to Chips Act 2.0's sovereign compute/AI design goals; investors include NXP, CEA, Safran, Bpifrance.
uncertain: FY2025 consolidated revenue ~EUR16.45M not like-for-like vs FY2024 (~EUR24.8M) after the Data Acceleration business was sold Feb 2025; growth left null
Employees ~215 reduced after the Feb 2025 divestiture

companies.name: Axelera AI
companies.description: Eindhoven-based fabless startup developing energy-efficient AI inference chips (Metis AIPU, the Europa chip) for edge and data-centre workloads using in-memory compute and RISC-V.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's best-funded AI-chip startup and a flagship of EU efforts to build sovereign AI inference silicon; central to Chips Act 2.0 ambitions.
uncertain: Feb 2026 round ~$250M; total ~$450M incl. debt/grants not cleanly confirmed in EUR — left null
Valuation undisclosed (press implies $1-2B)

companies.name: Black Semiconductor
companies.description: Aachen-based deep-tech (RWTH Aachen spin-out) developing graphene-based photonic interconnects for high-bandwidth optical chip-to-chip/chiplet communication on conventional semiconductor platforms.
companies.sovereignty_role: Targets a strategic gap in advanced packaging/interconnect; largely backed by the German federal government and NRW, building an EU graphene-photonics pilot line — aligned with Chips Act 2.0 advanced-packaging/materials goals.
uncertain: EUR254.4M (Jun 2024) is mostly public funding; ~EUR25.7M private

companies.name: VSORA
companies.description: Meudon-based fabless company designing high-performance AI inference chips (chiplet-based Jotunn8) and DSP/AI silicon for data-centre inference, autonomous driving and edge AI.
companies.sovereignty_role: An EU-HQ designer of high-end AI inference accelerators positioned as a European alternative to US data-centre inference silicon; backed by the EIC and France 2030.
uncertain: Apr 2025 round EUR40M; cumulative funding not cleanly aggregated — left null
Valuation undisclosed

companies.name: Aledia
companies.description: Grenoble-based CEA-Leti spin-off developing 3D GaN-on-silicon nanowire microLED technology and microdisplays for AR and next-gen displays, on 200/300mm silicon wafers.
companies.sovereignty_role: Brings advanced GaN/microLED display semiconductor manufacturing onto silicon within the EU — supporting Chips Act 2.0 photonics/advanced-materials goals; investors include Intel and Ikea (Ingka).
uncertain: ~$600M raised cumulatively reported; USD/EUR mixed, not precisely confirmed — total left null

companies.name: GreenWaves Technologies
companies.description: Grenoble-based fabless startup designing ultra-low-power RISC-V-based AI/DSP application processors (GAP8/GAP9) for battery-operated edge and IoT devices.
companies.sovereignty_role: An EU-HQ fabless designer building on open RISC-V for sovereign, energy-efficient edge-AI silicon; backed by Soitec, Thales, Innovacom and the French MoD's Definvest.
uncertain: ~EUR50M total raised reported; more recent funding not confirmed
Valuation undisclosed

companies.name: Nexperia
companies.description: Nijmegen-based IDM (former NXP Standard Products, spun out 2017) producing very high volumes of discrete, logic and MOSFET/power semiconductors for automotive and industrial markets, with fabs in Hamburg and Manchester.
companies.sovereignty_role: A major EU-based high-volume chipmaker critical to European automotive/industrial supply chains; in Oct 2025 the Dutch government invoked the Goods Availability Act to take control of its governance — a central EU economic-security case for Chips Act 2.0.
uncertain: EU-HQ but ultimately Chinese-owned (Wingtech, since 2019); under Dutch government control over governance since Oct 2025
Subsidiary, not separately listed; 2024 revenue ~$2.06B, ~12,500 staff globally (not all EU)

companies.name: PSI Software SE
companies.description: Berlin-based software company and European leader in energy management systems for electricity, gas, heat and water — grid control (SCADA/EMS/DMS), virtual power plant, energy trading and storage optimization software.
companies.sovereignty_role: Core EU supplier of network control and optimization software used by TSOs and DSOs — directly underpinning the grid digitalisation and AI-driven grid management the EU roadmap targets.
uncertain: Take-private in progress: Warburg Pincus reached ~83% by Jan 2026 and intends to delist from XETRA in Q1 2026 — PSAN.DE listing/financials may no longer be live; verify
2024 group revenue EUR260.8M spans 3 segments; only Energy Management is grid/energy software

companies.name: GridBeyond
companies.description: Dublin-HQ energy-technology company whose AI platform orchestrates distributed energy resources — C&I demand, batteries, grid-scale storage — for forecasting, optimization and automated dispatch into energy and balancing markets.
companies.sovereignty_role: AI-driven flexibility aggregation and automated energy trading integrating distributed assets into grid services — supports the roadmap's demand-response, DER and AI-for-grid-balancing goals.
uncertain: Dublin-HQ; also has UK/US offices
Total funding ~EUR80M approximate; Crunchbase ~$89.9M

companies.name: Voltalis
companies.description: Paris-based European leader in residential/commercial demand-side response, running a platform connecting over 1.5 million appliances to dynamically reduce and shift electricity consumption in real time.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operates one of Europe's largest distributed demand-response/flexibility platforms — the consumer-side flexibility the roadmap identifies as essential for AI-balanced grids.
uncertain: ~EUR215M is largely project/debt financing, not equity; no public equity valuation
Majority-owned by infrastructure investor Meridiam

companies.name: Enode
companies.description: Oslo-based SaaS offering a single API to connect and control distributed energy hardware — EVs, chargers, solar inverters, home batteries, HVAC — enabling third parties to build flexibility products and VPPs.
companies.sovereignty_role: Supplies the interoperability/control layer for distributed energy resources — connective infrastructure the roadmap needs to mobilise consumer assets for flexibility markets and AI grid balancing.
uncertain: ~EUR16M converted from ~$17.4M; valuation undisclosed

companies.name: FlexiDAO
companies.description: Barcelona-based SaaS whose platform tracks electricity consumption, production and energy attribute certificates hourly to enable verifiable 24/7 carbon-free energy claims and CSRD-grade emissions reporting.
companies.sovereignty_role: Granular, auditable electricity-and-carbon traceability software underpinning hourly clean-energy matching — aligned with the roadmap's data-driven energy markets.
uncertain: EUR6.1M reflects 2022 Series A; cumulative may be higher, no reliable updated figure
Google and Microsoft are both investors and customers

companies.name: Dexter Energy
companies.description: Amsterdam-based climate-tech providing AI/ML forecasting and trade-optimization software that dispatches renewables and batteries across day-ahead, intraday and imbalance power markets.
companies.sovereignty_role: Applies AI to short-term power-market forecasting and automated asset control for wind, solar and storage — serves the roadmap's focus on AI for renewable integration and power-market optimisation.
uncertain: ~EUR36M converted from ~$39.6M cumulative (incl. EUR23M Series C, Jul 2025); valuation undisclosed

companies.name: Energy Pool
companies.description: France-based demand-response and flexibility aggregator whose DRMS software optimizes and aggregates industrial loads, distributed generation and storage from real-time frequency regulation to long-term planning.
companies.sovereignty_role: DRMS/flexibility-aggregation software for TSOs and DSOs to run demand-response programs and microgrids — the grid-flexibility orchestration the roadmap targets.
uncertain: Funding/valuation not reliably found; reported majority-owned by Mitsubishi Corporation (verify)
Operations span Europe, MENA and Asia — not all EU-based

companies.name: Nebius Group
companies.description: Amsterdam-HQ AI infrastructure company providing GPU-backed cloud compute, managed Kubernetes, storage and large-scale data-centre capacity across Europe and the US. Spun out of the former Yandex N.V. and relisted on NASDAQ in 2024.
companies.sovereignty_role: A rare EU-domiciled, publicly listed full-stack AI cloud / GPU operator building large-scale European data-centre capacity — directly relevant to CADA's goal of tripling EU cloud capacity and providing sovereign frontier-AI compute.
uncertain: FY2025 revenue USD529.8m (+479%); EUR ~489m approximate conversion
NL-incorporated but substantial US data-centre ops and US NASDAQ listing; Yandex heritage divested 2024

companies.name: Gcore
companies.description: Luxembourg-HQ edge AI, cloud, CDN and security provider with 180+ points of presence, offering NVIDIA-GPU cloud for AI training and inference.
companies.sovereignty_role: EU-domiciled GPU cloud and edge-AI infrastructure offering European AI compute/inference capacity — aligned with CADA's sovereign-cloud and frontier-AI push.
uncertain: Funding shown is the single $60M Series A (~EUR55M); valuation undisclosed
Third-party revenue estimates unaudited — left null

companies.name: plusserver
companies.description: Cologne-HQ German managed-cloud provider offering a GDPR-compliant OpenStack-based 'sovereign' cloud (pluscloud open) on the Sovereign Cloud Stack, with four German data centres. Founding member of Gaia-X.
companies.sovereignty_role: A flagship 'made in Germany' sovereign-cloud operator and Gaia-X founder, explicitly positioned on data sovereignty and open-source cloud — matching CADA's sovereign-cloud framework and open-source-first stance.
uncertain: PE-owned by BC Partners (acquired from GoDaddy 2017, ~EUR385-397M); not independent
Revenue from third-party estimators — left null

companies.name: Sesterce
companies.description: Marseille-HQ French GPU cloud provider focused on NVIDIA-GPU capacity for AI training and inference (formerly crypto-miner BiTech), building large French AI data-centre capacity incl. a EUR450m first-phase site in Valence.
companies.sovereignty_role: Positions itself as the reference cloud for sovereign AI in France, building domestic GPU data-centre capacity — aligned with CADA's data-centre buildout and sovereign frontier-AI compute.
uncertain: No verified equity round/valuation; the EUR450M (Valence) is announced capex, not capital raised — left null
Founded ~2018 (rebranded from BiTech)

companies.name: Pleias
companies.description: Paris-HQ AI lab (Station F) building small, energy-efficient LLMs trained exclusively on open data, and coordinator of Common Corpus (~2 trillion tokens). Models released under Apache 2.0.
companies.sovereignty_role: Develops EU-legislation-compliant, fully open-data foundation models emphasising traceability — embodying CADA's open-source-first stance and Europe's sovereign frontier-AI ambitions.
uncertain: No traditional VC funding found (Mozilla Builders Accelerator only) — left null
Early-stage; revenue/headcount not disclosed

companies.name: Technoprobe S.p.A.
companies.description: Technoprobe S.p.A., together with its subsidiaries, produces and sells electronic circuits in Italy, Asia, the United States, and rest of Europe. The company provides mechanical interfaces for electrical contacting of hybrid circuits and semiconductor devices; and engages in the design, development, and production of probe cards for testing the operation of chips. Its products are used in digital data and 5G, IoT, data center, automotive and self-driving car, telecommunications and media, industry and aerospace, and consumer electronics applications.
